This document explains how the CQRS (Com: This document explains how the CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) pattern is implemented in MBC CQRS Serverless.
Command service persists to DynamoDB wit: Command service persists to DynamoDB with optimistic locking
Domain events are published to SNS: Domain events are published to SNS
Data service queries DynamoDB or RDS: Data service queries DynamoDB or RDS
Adopting the CQRS pattern provides these: Adopting the CQRS pattern provides these benefits:
Read and write can be scaled independent: Read and write can be scaled independently
Optimize each side for its specific purp: Optimize each side for its specific purpose
Use different data models for reads and: Use different data models for reads and writes
Denormalize read models for fast queries: Denormalize read models for fast queries
Complete event history for audit trails: Complete event history for audit trails
Because `publishAsync` writes to the com: Because `publishAsync` writes to the command table and then returns immediately — before the SNS-triggered projector has updated the read store — there is a short window where a subsequent read will return stale data:
This is expected behaviour in an eventua: This is expected behaviour in an eventually-consistent system, but can cause confusing UX when a user creates/updates a record and immediately navigates to the list — only to see the previous state.
MBC CQRS Serverless v1.2.0 introduced a: MBC CQRS Serverless v1.2.0 introduced a session-based **Read-Your-Writes** layer that bridges this async window for the user who just issued the write:
When `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` is set, a: When `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` is set, after each `publishAsync` / `publishPartialUpdateAsync` call the `SessionService` writes a short-lived entry to a dedicated session table. The `Repository` class (which wraps `DataService`) automatically reads those entries and merges any pending commands into the query result — so the caller sees their own write immediately, even before the projector has run.
Written by `SessionService.put()` after: Written by `SessionService.put()` after every successful `publishAsync`; expires after `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` minutes
Drop-in replacement for `DataService` th: Drop-in replacement for `DataService` that transparently applies the RYW merge
Pending `create` commands are prepended;: Pending `create` commands are prepended; pending `delete` commands are filtered out; `update` / `partial-update` are applied on top of the read-store item
When `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` is unset,: When `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` is unset or set to a non-positive value, `SessionService.put()` is a no-op and `Repository` behaves identically to `DataService`
Read-Your-Writes support (`SessionServic: Read-Your-Writes support (`SessionService`, `Repository`) was added in [v1.2.0](/docs/changelog#v120). Enabling it requires setting `RYW_SESSION_TTL_MINUTES` and provisioning a session DynamoDB table.
See the [Read-Your-Writes implementation: See the [Read-Your-Writes implementation guide](/docs/command-service#read-your-writes) for setup steps and full API reference.
